# connectors.csv

**Generated by:** [`map_solutions_connectors_tables.py`](../map_solutions_connectors_tables.md)
**Granularity:** One row per connector (deduplicated by `connector_id`)
**Type:** Entity table

## Overview

Connector metadata for every Microsoft Sentinel data connector discovered in the `Solutions/` directory, enriched with a derived collection method, ingestion API, CCF capabilities, CLv1 status, marketplace publication status, and filter-field analysis of the connector's queries.

## Use Cases

- **Inventory of all connectors** â€” list every connector across all solutions with a single source of truth.
- **Filter by collection method** â€” find all AMA / CCF / CCF Push / Azure Function / MMA / REST / Native connectors (e.g. for migration planning).
- **Identify legacy ingestion** â€” query `is_clv1 == true` or `ingestion_api == "HTTP Data Collector API"` to find connectors that should be migrated to DCR/Log Ingestion API.
- **CCF feature audit** â€” use `ccf_capabilities` to find connectors using paging, OAuth2, push mode, etc.
- **Content-hub publication status** â€” `is_published` indicates whether the connector ships to the content hub: `true` when the parent solution is live on Azure Marketplace *and* the connector is referenced by the solution definition file; `false` for connectors discovered only in the `Data Connectors` folder but absent from the solution's `Solution_*.json` (they are retained for inventory but are not on the content hub).
- **Source-data filtering** â€” `filter_fields`, `event_vendor`, `event_product` reveal which vendor/product/event-class each connector targets.
- **Deprecation tracking** â€” `is_deprecated` + `deprecation_date` for clean-up planning.
- **Discovered vs declared** â€” `not_in_solution_json == true` flags connectors found via mainTemplate fallback or file scan that aren't listed in the Solution JSON. When the parent solution ships a working definition that references other connectors, such folder-only connectors are also forced to `is_published == false` (not on the content hub).
- **Kusto upload** â€” uploaded as `solution_analyzer_connectors_lookup` by `upload_to_kusto.py`.

## Columns

| Column | Description | Data Source |
|--------|-------------|-------------|
| `connector_id` | Unique connector identifier | Connector JSON `id` field, or generated from title |
| `connector_publisher` | Connector publisher name | Connector JSON `publisher` field |
| `connector_title` | Connector display title | Connector JSON `title` field |
| `connector_description` | Connector description | Connector JSON `descriptionMarkdown` field |
| `connector_instruction_steps` | Setup and configuration instructions (JSON) | Connector JSON `instructionSteps` field |
| `connector_permissions` | Required permissions (JSON) | Connector JSON `permissions` field |
| `connector_id_generated` | `true` if ID was auto-generated from title (ARM `[variables(...)]` reference) | Computed |
| `connector_files` | Semicolon-separated GitHub URLs to connector definition files | File system scan |
| `connector_readme_file` | Path to connector README file (if exists) | File system scan |
| `collection_method` | Collection method (AMA, MMA, CCF, CCF Push, CCF (Legacy), Azure Function, REST API, Native, Azure Diagnostics, Unknown) | Multi-pattern analysis â€” see [`map_solutions_connectors_tables.md` â€ş Collection Method Detection](../map_solutions_connectors_tables.md#collection-method-detection) |
| `collection_method_reason` | Explanation of how the collection method was determined | Computed |
| `event_vendor` | Semicolon-separated vendor values extracted from queries | KQL filter-field analysis |
| `event_product` | Semicolon-separated product values extracted from queries | KQL filter-field analysis |
| `event_vendor_product_by_table` | JSON mapping of tables â†’ vendor/product pairs | KQL filter-field analysis with table context |
| `filter_fields` | Pipe-separated `Table.Field op "value"` clauses extracted from connector queries. Predicates on raw documented columns are attributed to the owning table; predicates on fields defined via `\| extend` use the synthetic table `_Computed`. When a connector's query is just a vendor parser-function call (e.g. `ClarotyEvent`), the parser's own `filter_fields` are inherited so the connector reflects the parser's selection criteria. | See [`map_solutions_connectors_tables.md` â€ş Filter Fields Detection](../map_solutions_connectors_tables.md#filter-fields-detection) |
| `not_in_solution_json` | `true` if found by file scan / mainTemplate fallback but not listed in Solution JSON | Comparison |
| `solution_name` | Parent solution name | Solution folder |
| `is_deprecated` | `true` if title contains `[DEPRECATED]`, JSON `availability.status` is `0`, or the parent solution is deprecated | Pattern + inheritance |
| `deprecation_date` | Date string extracted from description when deprecated | Regex extraction; overridable |
| `is_published` | `true` if the connector ships to the content hub â€” parent solution published on Azure Marketplace **and** the connector referenced by the solution definition file. Forced to `false` for folder-only connectors that are absent from a working `Solution_*.json` (retained for inventory, not on the content hub) | Marketplace API + definition-file check |
| `dcr_definition_files` | Semicolon-separated GitHub URLs to companion DCR files associated with the connector (for example `*_DCR.json` or `dcr.json`) | File system scan |
| `ccf_config_file` | GitHub URL to the CCF poller/push config file (CCF / CCF Push only; empty for CCF Legacy) | File system scan |
| `ccf_capabilities` | Semicolon-separated CCF capabilities (auth type, paging, POST, push, etc.) | Parsed from CCF config JSON |
| `ingestion_api` | `Log Ingestion API`, `HTTP Data Collector API`, `Undetermined`, or empty (CCF/Native are platform-managed) | Multi-rule detection |
| `ingestion_api_reason` | Human-readable explanation of how `ingestion_api` was determined | Computed |
| `is_clv1` | `true` if any of the connector's tables use the legacy Custom Log V1 schema | CLv1 detection |
| `learn_doc_url` | Deep-link to the connector's section on the Microsoft Learn [`data-connectors-reference`](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/sentinel/data-connectors-reference) page (e.g. `â€¦#cisco-secure-endpoint-via-codeless-connector-framework`). Populated when the connector's title slugifies (GitHub-style) to an existing `<a name="â€¦">` anchor on that page, with `[Recommended]` / `[Preview]` prefixes and trailing `(Preview)` markers stripped. Empty when no anchor matches. | Anchor scrape of Learn page, cached as `data_connectors_reference_anchors.json` |
